{"step_by_step_thinking": "Tongue thrusting is a habit where the tongue pushes against the teeth during swallowing, which can lead to several dental issues. Spaced dentition, crowding and crossbite, and open bite are all common consequences of tongue thrusting. However, a flush terminal plane, which means the teeth meet evenly at the gum line, is not a typical feature of tongue thrusting. Instead, it is more associated with normal occlusion.", "answer_choice": "D"}
